channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Fluctuation-Induced Giant Magnetoresistance in Charge-Neutral Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): A. Levchenko, E. Kirkinis, and A. V. Andreev

The Johnson-Nyquist noise associated with the intrinsic conductivity of the electron liquid induces fluctuations of the electron density in charge-neutral graphene devices. In the presence of external electric and magnetic fields, the fluctuations of charge density and electric current induce a fluc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126301] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. Levchenko, E. Kirkinis, and A. V. Andreev

The Johnson-Nyquist noise associated with the intrinsic conductivity of the electron liquid induces fluctuations of the electron density in charge-neutral graphene devices. In the presence of external electric and magnetic fields, the fluctuations of charge density and electric current induce a fluc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126301] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Fluctuation-Induced Giant Magnetoresistance in Charge-Neutral Graphene
item.dc:creator: A. Levchenko, E. Kirkinis, and A. V. Andreev
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Biorthogonal Neural Network Approach to 2D Non-Hermitian Systems
item.description:
Author(s): Massimo Solinas, Brandon Barton, Yuxuan Zhang, Jannes Nys, and Juan Carrasquilla

Non-Hermitian (NH) quantum many-body systems exhibit a rich array of physical phenomena, including NH skin effects and exceptional points, that remain largely inaccessible to existing numerical techniques. In this Letter, we investigate the application of variational Monte Carlo and neural network w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126501] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Massimo Solinas, Brandon Barton, Yuxuan Zhang, Jannes Nys, and Juan Carrasquilla

Non-Hermitian (NH) quantum many-body systems exhibit a rich array of physical phenomena, including NH skin effects and exceptional points, that remain largely inaccessible to existing numerical techniques. In this Letter, we investigate the application of variational Monte Carlo and neural network w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126501] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Biorthogonal Neural Network Approach to 2D Non-Hermitian Systems
item.dc:creator: Massimo Solinas, Brandon Barton, Yuxuan Zhang, Jannes Nys, and Juan Carrasquilla
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Field-Free Electrical Switching of Perpendicular Magnetization via Domain-Wall-Free Textures in Ferrimagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Xueqiang Feng, Zhizhong Zhang, Yuze Xie, Zhenyi Zheng, Jinkai Wang, Zuojun Song, Bowen Yang, Kelian Lin, Guo Liu, Yu He, Bo Li, Weisheng Zhao, and Yue Zhang

Discovering and effectively leveraging nontrivial magnetic texture in magnetic materials is of great significance for developing next-generation spintronic devices. In this Letter, we demonstrate that solid-state hydrogen gating in rare-earth-transition-metal ferrimagnetic alloys can reversibly crea…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126701] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xueqiang Feng, Zhizhong Zhang, Yuze Xie, Zhenyi Zheng, Jinkai Wang, Zuojun Song, Bowen Yang, Kelian Lin, Guo Liu, Yu He, Bo Li, Weisheng Zhao, and Yue Zhang

Discovering and effectively leveraging nontrivial magnetic texture in magnetic materials is of great significance for developing next-generation spintronic devices. In this Letter, we demonstrate that solid-state hydrogen gating in rare-earth-transition-metal ferrimagnetic alloys can reversibly crea…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126701] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Field-Free Electrical Switching of Perpendicular Magnetization via Domain-Wall-Free Textures in Ferrimagnets
item.dc:creator: Xueqiang Feng, Zhizhong Zhang, Yuze Xie, Zhenyi Zheng, Jinkai Wang, Zuojun Song, Bowen Yang, Kelian Lin, Guo Liu, Yu He, Bo Li, Weisheng Zhao, and Yue Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Engineering Long-Range and Multibody Interactions via Global Kinetic Constraints
item.description:
Author(s): Runmin Wu, Bing Yang, Pieter W. Claeys, and Hongzheng Zhao

Long-range and multibody interactions are crucial for quantum simulation and quantum computation. Yet, their practical realization using elementary pairwise interactions remains an outstanding challenge. We propose an experimental scheme based on the Bose-Hubbard system with a periodic driving of th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 120401] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Runmin Wu, Bing Yang, Pieter W. Claeys, and Hongzheng Zhao

Long-range and multibody interactions are crucial for quantum simulation and quantum computation. Yet, their practical realization using elementary pairwise interactions remains an outstanding challenge. We propose an experimental scheme based on the Bose-Hubbard system with a periodic driving of th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 120401] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Engineering Long-Range and Multibody Interactions via Global Kinetic Constraints
item.dc:creator: Runmin Wu, Bing Yang, Pieter W. Claeys, and Hongzheng Zhao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Chirality-Induced Spin Currents in a Fermi Gas
item.description:
Author(s): Camen A. Royse and J. E. Thomas

We observe and model spin currents arising from chirality and effective spin-exchange interactions in a weakly interacting $^{6}\mathrm{Li}$ Fermi gas. Chirality is introduced by a static displacement between the center of the trapped atoms and the center of an applied magnetic bowl, which produces …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 123401] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Camen A. Royse and J. E. Thomas

We observe and model spin currents arising from chirality and effective spin-exchange interactions in a weakly interacting Li6 Fermi gas. Chirality is introduced by a static displacement between the center of the trapped atoms and the center of an applied magnetic bowl, which produces left- or right…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 123401] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Chirality-Induced Spin Currents in a Fermi Gas
item.dc:creator: Camen A. Royse and J. E. Thomas
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Revisiting Dissipation-Driven Phase Transition in a Josephson Junction
item.description:
Author(s): Diego Subero, Yu-Cheng Chang, Miguel Monteiro, Ze-Yan Chen, and Jukka P. Pekola

A low-frequency DC transport measurement on a Josephson junction and SQUID connected to a well-defined on-chip resistive environment shows a crossover from superconducting to insulating behavior when the environmental resistance exceeds the quantum value of RQ=h/4e2≈6.5 kΩ.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116301] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Diego Subero, Yu-Cheng Chang, Miguel Monteiro, Ze-Yan Chen, and Jukka P. Pekola

A low-frequency DC transport measurement on a Josephson junction and SQUID connected to a well-defined on-chip resistive environment shows a crossover from superconducting to insulating behavior when the environmental resistance exceeds the quantum value of RQ=h/4e2≈6.5 kΩ.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116301] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Revisiting Dissipation-Driven Phase Transition in a Josephson Junction
item.dc:creator: Diego Subero, Yu-Cheng Chang, Miguel Monteiro, Ze-Yan Chen, and Jukka P. Pekola
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Witnessing Spin-Orbital Entanglement Using Resonant Inelastic X-Ray Scattering
item.description:
Author(s): Zecheng Shen, Shuhan Ding, Zijun Zhao, Francesco A. Evangelista, and Yao Wang

Entanglement plays a central role in quantum technologies, yet its characterization and control in materials remain challenging. Recent developments in spectrum-based entanglement witnesses have enabled new strategies for quantifying many-body entanglement in macroscopic materials. Here, we develop …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116504] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zecheng Shen, Shuhan Ding, Zijun Zhao, Francesco A. Evangelista, and Yao Wang

Entanglement plays a central role in quantum technologies, yet its characterization and control in materials remain challenging. Recent developments in spectrum-based entanglement witnesses have enabled new strategies for quantifying many-body entanglement in macroscopic materials. Here, we develop …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116504] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Witnessing Spin-Orbital Entanglement Using Resonant Inelastic X-Ray Scattering
item.dc:creator: Zecheng Shen, Shuhan Ding, Zijun Zhao, Francesco A. Evangelista, and Yao Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Direct Raman Observation of the Quantum Metric in a Quantum Magnet
item.description:
Author(s): Chao-Fan Wang, Han Ge, Jun-Yang Chen, Liusuo Wu, Xiaobin Chen, Jia-Wei Mei, and Mingyuan Huang

The quantum geometric tensor unifies the Berry curvature (its imaginary part) and the quantum metric (its real part), yet Raman studies of chiral phonons have so far accessed only the former. We perform circularly polarized Raman spectroscopy on the quantum magnet ${\mathrm{K}}_{2}\mathrm{Co}({\math…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116605] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chao-Fan Wang, Han Ge, Jun-Yang Chen, Liusuo Wu, Xiaobin Chen, Jia-Wei Mei, and Mingyuan Huang

The quantum geometric tensor unifies the Berry curvature (its imaginary part) and the quantum metric (its real part), yet Raman studies of chiral phonons have so far accessed only the former. We perform circularly polarized Raman spectroscopy on the quantum magnet K2Co(SeO3)2, where the field-odd ch…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116605] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Direct Raman Observation of the Quantum Metric in a Quantum Magnet
item.dc:creator: Chao-Fan Wang, Han Ge, Jun-Yang Chen, Liusuo Wu, Xiaobin Chen, Jia-Wei Mei, and Mingyuan Huang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Net and Compensated Altermagnetism from Staggered Orbital Order: Layer-Dependent Spin Splitting in ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{n+1}{\mathrm{Cr}}_{n}{\mathrm{O}}_{3n+1}$
item.description:
Author(s): Quintin N. Meier, Alberto Carta, Claude Ederer, and Andrés Cano

Altermagnets are collinear antiferromagnets with non-relativistic spin splitting of the electronic states. We identify the Ruddlesden-Popper chromates $\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{r}}_{n+1}\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{r}}_{n}{\mathrm{O}}_{3n+1}$, including the perovskite ${\mathrm{SrCrO}}_{3}$, as candidate materials …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116705] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Quintin N. Meier, Alberto Carta, Claude Ederer, and Andrés Cano

Altermagnets are collinear antiferromagnets with non-relativistic spin splitting of the electronic states. We identify the Ruddlesden-Popper chromates Srn+1CrnO3n+1, including the perovskite SrCrO3, as candidate materials in which altermagnetism can emerge from spontaneous orbital ordering rather th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116705] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Net and Compensated Altermagnetism from Staggered Orbital Order: Layer-Dependent Spin Splitting in ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{n+1}{\mathrm{Cr}}_{n}{\mathrm{O}}_{3n+1}$
item.dc:creator: Quintin N. Meier, Alberto Carta, Claude Ederer, and Andrés Cano
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Surface Acoustic Wave Driven Acoustic Spin Splitter in $d$-Wave Altermagnetic Thin Films
item.description:
Author(s): Pieter M. Gunnink, Jairo Sinova, and Alexander Mook

The generation of spin currents is a key challenge in the field of spintronics. We propose using surface acoustic waves (SAWs) to generate spin currents in altermagnetic thin films, thereby realizing an acoustic spin splitter. Altermagnets, characterized by spin-polarized electrons and magnons, prov…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116706] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pieter M. Gunnink, Jairo Sinova, and Alexander Mook

The generation of spin currents is a key challenge in the field of spintronics. We propose using surface acoustic waves (SAWs) to generate spin currents in altermagnetic thin films, thereby realizing an acoustic spin splitter. Altermagnets, characterized by spin-polarized electrons and magnons, prov…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116706] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Surface Acoustic Wave Driven Acoustic Spin Splitter in $d$-Wave Altermagnetic Thin Films
item.dc:creator: Pieter M. Gunnink, Jairo Sinova, and Alexander Mook
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Exceptional Optical Phonon Coherence in Enriched Cubic Boron Arsenide via Suppression of Three-Phonon Scattering
item.description:
Author(s): Tong Lin, Fengjiao Pan, Gaihua Ye, Sanjna Sukumaran, Cynthia Nnokwe, Ange Benise Niyikiza, William A. Smith, Stephen B. Bayne, Rui He, Zhifeng Ren, and Hanyu Zhu

Cubic boron arsenide (BAs) is a promising semiconductor for next-generation electronics due to its outstanding ambipolar mobility and thermal conductivity, the latter of which is attributed to the suppression of three-phonon scattering. However, precisely accounting for different high-order anharmon…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116903] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tong Lin, Fengjiao Pan, Gaihua Ye, Sanjna Sukumaran, Cynthia Nnokwe, Ange Benise Niyikiza, William A. Smith, Stephen B. Bayne, Rui He, Zhifeng Ren, and Hanyu Zhu

Cubic boron arsenide (BAs) is a promising semiconductor for next-generation electronics due to its outstanding ambipolar mobility and thermal conductivity, the latter of which is attributed to the suppression of three-phonon scattering. However, precisely accounting for different high-order anharmon…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116903] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Exceptional Optical Phonon Coherence in Enriched Cubic Boron Arsenide via Suppression of Three-Phonon Scattering
item.dc:creator: Tong Lin, Fengjiao Pan, Gaihua Ye, Sanjna Sukumaran, Cynthia Nnokwe, Ange Benise Niyikiza, William A. Smith, Stephen B. Bayne, Rui He, Zhifeng Ren, and Hanyu Zhu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Matrix Product States and First Quantization
item.description:
Author(s): Jheng-Wei Li and Xavier Waintal

Common wisdom says that the entanglement of fermionic systems can be low in the second quantization formalism but is extremely large in the first quantization. Hence matrix product state (MPS) methods based on moderate entanglement have been overwhelmingly formulated in the second quantization. Here…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116503] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jheng-Wei Li and Xavier Waintal

Common wisdom says that the entanglement of fermionic systems can be low in the second quantization formalism but is extremely large in the first quantization. Hence matrix product state (MPS) methods based on moderate entanglement have been overwhelmingly formulated in the second quantization. Here…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116503] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Matrix Product States and First Quantization
item.dc:creator: Jheng-Wei Li and Xavier Waintal
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Universal Decay of Mutual Information and Conditional Mutual Information in Gapped Pure- and Mixed-State Quantum Matter
item.description:
Author(s): Jinmin Yi, Kangle Li, Chuan Liu, Zixuan Li, and Liujun Zou

For spin and fermionic systems in any spatial dimension, we establish that the superpolynomial decay behavior of mutual information and conditional mutual information is a universal property of gapped pure- and mixed-state phases; i.e., all systems in such a phase possess this property if one system…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116604] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jinmin Yi, Kangle Li, Chuan Liu, Zixuan Li, and Liujun Zou

For spin and fermionic systems in any spatial dimension, we establish that the superpolynomial decay behavior of mutual information and conditional mutual information is a universal property of gapped pure- and mixed-state phases; i.e., all systems in such a phase possess this property if one system…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116604] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Universal Decay of Mutual Information and Conditional Mutual Information in Gapped Pure- and Mixed-State Quantum Matter
item.dc:creator: Jinmin Yi, Kangle Li, Chuan Liu, Zixuan Li, and Liujun Zou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Observation of the Magnon Hall Magnetoresistance Effect
item.description:
Author(s): Shuan-Cheng Mai, Po-Hsun Wu, Chao-Wei Chen, Ssu-Yen Huang, Xin Fan, and Danru Qu

Interconversion between charge and spin currents via spin-orbit coupling underpins spin orbitronics. Magnons, which are the quanta of spin waves, can exchange angular momentum with conduction-electron spins through spin-flip scattering, suggesting a direct route for charge-to-magnon conversion. Here…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116704] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuan-Cheng Mai, Po-Hsun Wu, Chao-Wei Chen, Ssu-Yen Huang, Xin Fan, and Danru Qu

Interconversion between charge and spin currents via spin-orbit coupling underpins spin orbitronics. Magnons, which are the quanta of spin waves, can exchange angular momentum with conduction-electron spins through spin-flip scattering, suggesting a direct route for charge-to-magnon conversion. Here…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116704] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Observation of the Magnon Hall Magnetoresistance Effect
item.dc:creator: Shuan-Cheng Mai, Po-Hsun Wu, Chao-Wei Chen, Ssu-Yen Huang, Xin Fan, and Danru Qu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Coercivity Landscape Characterizes Dynamic Hysteresis
item.description:
Author(s): Miao Chen, Xiu-Hua Zhao, and Yu-Han Ma

The landscape of coercivity helps identify the scaling laws of hysteresis across dynamical regimes.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 117102] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Miao Chen, Xiu-Hua Zhao, and Yu-Han Ma

The landscape of coercivity helps identify the scaling laws of hysteresis across dynamical regimes.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 117102] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Coercivity Landscape Characterizes Dynamic Hysteresis
item.dc:creator: Miao Chen, Xiu-Hua Zhao, and Yu-Han Ma

item.dc:subject: Statistical Physics; Classical, Nonlinear, and Complex Systems
item.prism:section: Statistical Physics; Classical, Nonlinear, and Complex Systems

item.title: Quantum Time Crystal Clock and Its Performance
item.description:
Author(s): Ludmila Viotti, Marcus Huber, Rosario Fazio, and Gonzalo Manzano

New theoretical work shows how an unusual state of matter that oscillates between spin states could be used in timekeeping.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 110401] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ludmila Viotti, Marcus Huber, Rosario Fazio, and Gonzalo Manzano

New theoretical work shows how an unusual state of matter that oscillates between spin states could be used in timekeeping.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 110401] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Time Crystal Clock and Its Performance
item.dc:creator: Ludmila Viotti, Marcus Huber, Rosario Fazio, and Gonzalo Manzano
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Imaginary Gauge Potentials in a Non-Hermitian Spin-Orbit Coupled Quantum Gas
item.description:
Author(s): J. Tao, E. D. Mercado-Gutierrez, M. Zhao, and I. B. Spielman

A continuum analog of the Hatano-Nelson model using a homogeneous spin-orbit-coupled Bose-Einstein condensate shows that repulsive interactions enhance self-acceleration while suppressing the non-Hermitian skin effect.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 113401] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. Tao, E. D. Mercado-Gutierrez, M. Zhao, and I. B. Spielman

A continuum analog of the Hatano-Nelson model using a homogeneous spin-orbit-coupled Bose-Einstein condensate shows that repulsive interactions enhance self-acceleration while suppressing the non-Hermitian skin effect.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 113401] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Imaginary Gauge Potentials in a Non-Hermitian Spin-Orbit Coupled Quantum Gas
item.dc:creator: J. Tao, E. D. Mercado-Gutierrez, M. Zhao, and I. B. Spielman
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Goldstone-Mediated Polar Instability in Hexagonal Barium Titanate
item.description:
Author(s): Struan Simpson, Urmimala Dey, Robin Sjökvist, Jonathan Wright, Clemens Ritter, Richard Beanland, Nicholas C. Bristowe, and Mark S. Senn

We discover a rare structural manifestation of the Goldstone paradigm in a hexagonal polytype of the prototypical ferroelectric ${\mathrm{BaTiO}}_{3}$. First-principles calculations confirm the Goldstone character of the order parameter, while our high-resolution diffraction measurements unveil an u…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116101] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Struan Simpson, Urmimala Dey, Robin Sjökvist, Jonathan Wright, Clemens Ritter, Richard Beanland, Nicholas C. Bristowe, and Mark S. Senn

We discover a rare structural manifestation of the Goldstone paradigm in a hexagonal polytype of the prototypical ferroelectric BaTiO3. First-principles calculations confirm the Goldstone character of the order parameter, while our high-resolution diffraction measurements unveil an unusual reentrant…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116101] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Goldstone-Mediated Polar Instability in Hexagonal Barium Titanate
item.dc:creator: Struan Simpson, Urmimala Dey, Robin Sjökvist, Jonathan Wright, Clemens Ritter, Richard Beanland, Nicholas C. Bristowe, and Mark S. Senn
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Structurally Driven, Reversible Topological Phase Transition in a Distorted Square Net Material
item.description:
Author(s): Xian P. Yang, Chia-Hsiu Hsu, Gokul Acharya, Junyi Zhang, Md Shafayat Hossain, Tyler A. Cochran, Bimal Neupane, Zi-Jia Cheng, Santosh Karki Chhetri, Byunghoon Kim, Shiyuan Gao, Yu-Xiao Jiang, Maksim Litskevich, Jian Wang, Yuanxi Wang, Jin Hu, and M. Zahid Hasan

Topological materials hold immense promise for exhibiting exotic quantum phenomena, yet achieving controllable topological phase transitions remains challenging. Here, we demonstrate a structurally driven, reversible topological phase transition in the distorted square net material GdPS, induced via…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116603] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xian P. Yang, Chia-Hsiu Hsu, Gokul Acharya, Junyi Zhang, Md Shafayat Hossain, Tyler A. Cochran, Bimal Neupane, Zi-Jia Cheng, Santosh Karki Chhetri, Byunghoon Kim, Shiyuan Gao, Yu-Xiao Jiang, Maksim Litskevich, Jian Wang, Yuanxi Wang, Jin Hu, and M. Zahid Hasan

Topological materials hold immense promise for exhibiting exotic quantum phenomena, yet achieving controllable topological phase transitions remains challenging. Here, we demonstrate a structurally driven, reversible topological phase transition in the distorted square net material GdPS, induced via…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116603] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Structurally Driven, Reversible Topological Phase Transition in a Distorted Square Net Material
item.dc:creator: Xian P. Yang, Chia-Hsiu Hsu, Gokul Acharya, Junyi Zhang, Md Shafayat Hossain, Tyler A. Cochran, Bimal Neupane, Zi-Jia Cheng, Santosh Karki Chhetri, Byunghoon Kim, Shiyuan Gao, Yu-Xiao Jiang, Maksim Litskevich, Jian Wang, Yuanxi Wang, Jin Hu, and M. Zahid Hasan

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: All-Dielectric Metaphotonics for Advanced THz Control of Spins
item.description:
Author(s): Lucas van Gerven, Daria O. Ignatyeva, Daniil V. Konkov, V. Bilyk, T. Metzger, Denis M. Krichevsky, Svetlana A. Evstigneeva, Petr M. Vetoshko, Vladimir I. Belotelov, and Aleksei V. Kimel

A dielectric THz metasurface reshapes and amplifies ultrafast electromagnetic fields to give unprecedented 3D control over spin dynamics.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116703] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lucas van Gerven, Daria O. Ignatyeva, Daniil V. Konkov, V. Bilyk, T. Metzger, Denis M. Krichevsky, Svetlana A. Evstigneeva, Petr M. Vetoshko, Vladimir I. Belotelov, and Aleksei V. Kimel

A dielectric THz metasurface reshapes and amplifies ultrafast electromagnetic fields to give unprecedented 3D control over spin dynamics.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116703] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: All-Dielectric Metaphotonics for Advanced THz Control of Spins
item.dc:creator: Lucas van Gerven, Daria O. Ignatyeva, Daniil V. Konkov, V. Bilyk, T. Metzger, Denis M. Krichevsky, Svetlana A. Evstigneeva, Petr M. Vetoshko, Vladimir I. Belotelov, and Aleksei V. Kimel

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Electrical Switching of the Berry Phase in Bernal Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots
item.description:
Author(s): Ke Lv, Qiang Cheng, Yu-Chen Zhuang, Chen-Yue Hao, Xiao-Ya Wang, Ya-Xin Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Ya-Ning Ren, Qing-Feng Sun, and Lin He

The Berry phase—a fundamental quantum geometric phase given by the integral of the Berry curvature over a closed trajectory in momentum space—underlies a wide range of quantum topological phenomena. Recent advances have demonstrated the potential of tuning the Berry phase to access exotic quantum st…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116201] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ke Lv, Qiang Cheng, Yu-Chen Zhuang, Chen-Yue Hao, Xiao-Ya Wang, Ya-Xin Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Ya-Ning Ren, Qing-Feng Sun, and Lin He

The Berry phase—a fundamental quantum geometric phase given by the integral of the Berry curvature over a closed trajectory in momentum space—underlies a wide range of quantum topological phenomena. Recent advances have demonstrated the potential of tuning the Berry phase to access exotic quantum st…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116201] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Electrical Switching of the Berry Phase in Bernal Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots
item.dc:creator: Ke Lv, Qiang Cheng, Yu-Chen Zhuang, Chen-Yue Hao, Xiao-Ya Wang, Ya-Xin Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Ya-Ning Ren, Qing-Feng Sun, and Lin He
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Incompressible Quantum Hall Liquid on the Four-Dimensional Sphere
item.description:
Author(s): Junwen Zhao, Xue Meng, Wei Zhu, and Congjun Wu

The quantum Hall effect (QHE) is a cornerstone of topological physics, inspiring extensive explorations of its high-dimensional generalizations such as experimental realizations in synthetic systems including cold atoms, photonic lattices, and metamaterials. However, the many-body effect in the high…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116501] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Junwen Zhao, Xue Meng, Wei Zhu, and Congjun Wu

The quantum Hall effect (QHE) is a cornerstone of topological physics, inspiring extensive explorations of its high-dimensional generalizations such as experimental realizations in synthetic systems including cold atoms, photonic lattices, and metamaterials. However, the many-body effect in the high…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116501] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Incompressible Quantum Hall Liquid on the Four-Dimensional Sphere
item.dc:creator: Junwen Zhao, Xue Meng, Wei Zhu, and Congjun Wu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Non-Hermiticity Induced Universal Anomalies in Kondo Conductance
item.description:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

Strong correlation, when combined with dissipation in open systems, can lead to a variety of exotic quantum phenomena. Here, we study nontrivial interplays between non-Fermi liquid behaviors emerging from strong correlation and non-Hermiticity arising from open systems. We propose a practical physic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116502] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

Strong correlation, when combined with dissipation in open systems, can lead to a variety of exotic quantum phenomena. Here, we study nontrivial interplays between non-Fermi liquid behaviors emerging from strong correlation and non-Hermiticity arising from open systems. We propose a practical physic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116502] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Non-Hermiticity Induced Universal Anomalies in Kondo Conductance
item.dc:creator: Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Isoperimetric Inequalities in Quantum Geometry
item.description:
Author(s): Praveen Pai and Fan Zhang

The Berry phase and quantum distance characterize the geometry of wave functions in Hilbert space. We uncover a quantum analog of the classical isoperimetric inequality, revealing a fundamental link between the Berry phase and quantum distance along closed paths. For two-band systems, we establish a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116601] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Praveen Pai and Fan Zhang

The Berry phase and quantum distance characterize the geometry of wave functions in Hilbert space. We uncover a quantum analog of the classical isoperimetric inequality, revealing a fundamental link between the Berry phase and quantum distance along closed paths. For two-band systems, we establish a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116601] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Isoperimetric Inequalities in Quantum Geometry
item.dc:creator: Praveen Pai and Fan Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Geometric Inequality and Its Classical Wave Verification
item.description:
Author(s): Tingzhi Liu (刘亭志), Qicheng Zhang (张起成), and Chunyin Qiu (邱春印)

The study of the geometric properties of quantum states in Hilbert space—particularly through the lens of the quantum geometric tensor (QGT)—has profoundly advanced the fields of condensed matter physics and materials science. The real and imaginary parts of the QGT, the quantum metric and Berry cur…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116602] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tingzhi Liu (刘亭志), Qicheng Zhang (张起成), and Chunyin Qiu (邱春印)

The study of the geometric properties of quantum states in Hilbert space—particularly through the lens of the quantum geometric tensor (QGT)—has profoundly advanced the fields of condensed matter physics and materials science. The real and imaginary parts of the QGT, the quantum metric and Berry cur…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116602] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Geometric Inequality and Its Classical Wave Verification
item.dc:creator: Tingzhi Liu (刘亭志), Qicheng Zhang (张起成), and Chunyin Qiu (邱春印)

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spin-Polarized Josephson Supercurrent in Nodeless Altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Chuang Li, Jin-Xing Hou, Fu-Chun Zhang, Song-Bo Zhang, and Lun-Hui Hu

Long-range propagation of equal-spin triplet Cooper pairs typically occurs in ferromagnet-$s$-wave superconductor junctions, where net magnetization plays a crucial role. Here, we propose a fundamentally different scenario in which Josephson supercurrents mediated exclusively by spin-triplet pairing…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116701] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chuang Li, Jin-Xing Hou, Fu-Chun Zhang, Song-Bo Zhang, and Lun-Hui Hu

Long-range propagation of equal-spin triplet Cooper pairs typically occurs in ferromagnet-s-wave superconductor junctions, where net magnetization plays a crucial role. Here, we propose a fundamentally different scenario in which Josephson supercurrents mediated exclusively by spin-triplet pairings …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116701] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-Polarized Josephson Supercurrent in Nodeless Altermagnets

item.title: Unified Description of Spin-Lattice Coupling and Thermodynamics in the Pyrochlore Heisenberg Antiferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Masaki Gen, Hidemaro Suwa, Shusaku Imajo, Chao Dong, Hiroaki Ueda, Makoto Tachibana, Akihiko Ikeda, Koichi Kindo, and Yoshimitsu Kohama

We study an extended model to describe the spin-lattice coupling, incorporating individual vibrations of bonds and atomic sites alongside distance-dependent exchange interactions. The proposed spin Hamiltonian can be effectively considered as an interpolation between two well-established minimum mod…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116702] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Masaki Gen, Hidemaro Suwa, Shusaku Imajo, Chao Dong, Hiroaki Ueda, Makoto Tachibana, Akihiko Ikeda, Koichi Kindo, and Yoshimitsu Kohama

We study an extended model to describe the spin-lattice coupling, incorporating individual vibrations of bonds and atomic sites alongside distance-dependent exchange interactions. The proposed spin Hamiltonian can be effectively considered as an interpolation between two well-established minimum mod…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116702] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Unified Description of Spin-Lattice Coupling and Thermodynamics in the Pyrochlore Heisenberg Antiferromagnet

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: In-Plane Optically Tunable Magnetic States in 2D Materials via Tailored Femtosecond Laser Driving
item.description:
Author(s): Shuang Liu, Oren Cohen, Peng Chen, and Ofer Neufeld

It is well established that light can control magnetism in matter, e.g. via the inverse Faraday effect or ultrafast demagnetization. However, such control is typically limited to magnetization transverse to light’s polarization plane or out-of-plane magnetism in two-dimensional (2D) materials, while…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116901] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuang Liu, Oren Cohen, Peng Chen, and Ofer Neufeld

It is well established that light can control magnetism in matter, e.g. via the inverse Faraday effect or ultrafast demagnetization. However, such control is typically limited to magnetization transverse to light’s polarization plane or out-of-plane magnetism in two-dimensional (2D) materials, while…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116901] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: In-Plane Optically Tunable Magnetic States in 2D Materials via Tailored Femtosecond Laser Driving
item.dc:creator: Shuang Liu, Oren Cohen, Peng Chen, and Ofer Neufeld
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Droplets of Light in Semiconductor Microcavities
item.description:
Author(s): Matteo Caldara, Olivier Bleu, Francesca Maria Marchetti, Jesper Levinsen, and Meera M. Parish

Quantum droplets are dilute self-bound configurations of bosons that result from the balance between a mean-field attraction and a repulsion induced by quantum fluctuations. Such droplets have been successfully realized in cold atomic gases and represent a signature of their quantum nature. Here, we…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116902] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Matteo Caldara, Olivier Bleu, Francesca Maria Marchetti, Jesper Levinsen, and Meera M. Parish

Quantum droplets are dilute self-bound configurations of bosons that result from the balance between a mean-field attraction and a repulsion induced by quantum fluctuations. Such droplets have been successfully realized in cold atomic gases and represent a signature of their quantum nature. Here, we…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116902] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Droplets of Light in Semiconductor Microcavities
item.dc:creator: Matteo Caldara, Olivier Bleu, Francesca Maria Marchetti, Jesper Levinsen, and Meera M. Parish

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Readout Sweet Spots for Spin Qubits with Strong Spin-Orbit Interaction
item.description:
Author(s): Domonkos Svastits, Bence Hetényi, Gábor Széchenyi, James Wootton, Daniel Loss, Stefano Bosco, and András Pályi

Qubit readout schemes often deviate from ideal projective measurements, introducing critical issues that limit quantum computing performance. In this Letter, we model charge-sensing-based readout for semiconductor spin qubits in double quantum dots, and identify key error mechanisms caused by the ba…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 117001] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Domonkos Svastits, Bence Hetényi, Gábor Széchenyi, James Wootton, Daniel Loss, Stefano Bosco, and András Pályi

Qubit readout schemes often deviate from ideal projective measurements, introducing critical issues that limit quantum computing performance. In this Letter, we model charge-sensing-based readout for semiconductor spin qubits in double quantum dots, and identify key error mechanisms caused by the ba…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 117001] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Readout Sweet Spots for Spin Qubits with Strong Spin-Orbit Interaction
item.dc:creator: Domonkos Svastits, Bence Hetényi, Gábor Széchenyi, James Wootton, Daniel Loss, Stefano Bosco, and András Pályi

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Anisotropic Upper Critical Field beyond the Pauli Limit in a Nickelate Superconductor: Evidence for a Quantum Fluctuation Driven State
item.description:
Author(s): Xue-Yan Wang, Cheng-Xue Chen, Zi-Ming Mai, Qiang Zhao, Wen-Long Yang, Fang-Hui Zhu, Mei-Ling Yan, Rui-Fen Dou, Chang-Min Xiong, Haiwen Liu, and Jia-Cai Nie

High-field magnetotransport measurements of a nickelate superconductor up to 44 T reveal an anisotropic quantum Griffiths singularity, establishing disorder-enhanced quantum fluctuations as an intrinsic mechanism for protecting superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106002] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xue-Yan Wang, Cheng-Xue Chen, Zi-Ming Mai, Qiang Zhao, Wen-Long Yang, Fang-Hui Zhu, Mei-Ling Yan, Rui-Fen Dou, Chang-Min Xiong, Haiwen Liu, and Jia-Cai Nie

High-field magnetotransport measurements of a nickelate superconductor up to 44 T reveal an anisotropic quantum Griffiths singularity, establishing disorder-enhanced quantum fluctuations as an intrinsic mechanism for protecting superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106002] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Anisotropic Upper Critical Field beyond the Pauli Limit in a Nickelate Superconductor: Evidence for a Quantum Fluctuation Driven State
item.dc:creator: Xue-Yan Wang, Cheng-Xue Chen, Zi-Ming Mai, Qiang Zhao, Wen-Long Yang, Fang-Hui Zhu, Mei-Ling Yan, Rui-Fen Dou, Chang-Min Xiong, Haiwen Liu, and Jia-Cai Nie
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Interlayer Exciton Condensates between Second Landau Level Orbitals in Double Bilayer Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Zeyu Hao, A. M. Zimmerman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Philip Kim

Using Coulomb drag as an unambiguous probe of interlayer correlation reveals the first direct quantized drag evidence of interlayer exciton condensation beyond N = 0 in double bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106505] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zeyu Hao, A. M. Zimmerman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Philip Kim

Using Coulomb drag as an unambiguous probe of interlayer correlation reveals the first direct quantized drag evidence of interlayer exciton condensation beyond N = 0 in double bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106505] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Interlayer Exciton Condensates between Second Landau Level Orbitals in Double Bilayer Graphene
item.dc:creator: Zeyu Hao, A. M. Zimmerman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Philip Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Algebraic Loop Liquid in the Pyrochlore ${\mathrm{CsNiCrF}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): A. Hemmatzade, C. Balz, J. Ollivier, E. Lhotel, N. Colonna, A. dos Santos Rufino, G. J. Nilsen, Ø. S. Fjellvåg, M. Medarde, S. T. Bramwell, M. J. Harris, P. M. Derlet, and T. Fennell

The $β$-pyrochlore ${\mathrm{CsNiCrF}}_{6}$ is a charge ice—each tetrahedron of the pyrochlore lattice is occupied by two ${\mathrm{Ni}}^{2+}$ and two ${\mathrm{Cr}}^{3+}$ according to an ice rule, resulting in fully packed same-species loops with power-law length distribution. Using inelastic neutr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106701] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. Hemmatzade, C. Balz, J. Ollivier, E. Lhotel, N. Colonna, A. dos Santos Rufino, G. J. Nilsen, Ø. S. Fjellvåg, M. Medarde, S. T. Bramwell, M. J. Harris, P. M. Derlet, and T. Fennell

The β-pyrochlore CsNiCrF6 is a charge ice—each tetrahedron of the pyrochlore lattice is occupied by two Ni2+ and two Cr3+ according to an ice rule, resulting in fully packed same-species loops with power-law length distribution. Using inelastic neutron scattering and numerical simulations, we show t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106701] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Algebraic Loop Liquid in the Pyrochlore ${\mathrm{CsNiCrF}}_{6}$
item.dc:creator: A. Hemmatzade, C. Balz, J. Ollivier, E. Lhotel, N. Colonna, A. dos Santos Rufino, G. J. Nilsen, Ø. S. Fjellvåg, M. Medarde, S. T. Bramwell, M. J. Harris, P. M. Derlet, and T. Fennell
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: First-Principles Calculation of the Shift Current Bulk Photovoltaic Effect from Carrier Recombination
item.description:
Author(s): Aaron M. Schankler, Yutian Bao, and Andrew M. Rappe

The bulk photovoltaic effect, which occurs in single-phase, noncentrosymmetric materials, is characterized by two primary mechanisms—the ballistic current and the shift current—that each generate dc current during carrier excitation. The excitation shift current in particular has intriguing properti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106904] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Aaron M. Schankler, Yutian Bao, and Andrew M. Rappe

The bulk photovoltaic effect, which occurs in single-phase, noncentrosymmetric materials, is characterized by two primary mechanisms—the ballistic current and the shift current—that each generate dc current during carrier excitation. The excitation shift current in particular has intriguing properti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106904] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: First-Principles Calculation of the Shift Current Bulk Photovoltaic Effect from Carrier Recombination
item.dc:creator: Aaron M. Schankler, Yutian Bao, and Andrew M. Rappe
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nonlocal Correlation Effects in dc and Optical Conductivity of the Hubbard Model
item.description:
Author(s): Nagamalleswararao Dasari, Hugo U. R. Strand, Martin Eckstein, Alexander I. Lichtenstein, and Evgeny A. Stepanov

Conductivity is one of the most direct probes of electronic systems, yet its theoretical description remains challenging in the presence of strong nonlocal correlations. In this Letter, we analyze the conductivity of the half-filled single-band Hubbard model and identify the role of spatial correlat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106905] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nagamalleswararao Dasari, Hugo U. R. Strand, Martin Eckstein, Alexander I. Lichtenstein, and Evgeny A. Stepanov

Conductivity is one of the most direct probes of electronic systems, yet its theoretical description remains challenging in the presence of strong nonlocal correlations. In this Letter, we analyze the conductivity of the half-filled single-band Hubbard model and identify the role of spatial correlat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106905] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonlocal Correlation Effects in dc and Optical Conductivity of the Hubbard Model
item.dc:creator: Nagamalleswararao Dasari, Hugo U. R. Strand, Martin Eckstein, Alexander I. Lichtenstein, and Evgeny A. Stepanov
